An anonymous function can be crafted using the lambda keyword. This function will check if a number is prime or composite. The modulus operator is used to test divisibility. The status of the number is determined through this process. The function returns the status of the input number.

* * *

The function iterates through possible divisors up to the square root of the number. If any divisor divides the number evenly, the status is composite. If no such divisor exists, the status is prime. This logic ensures accurate classification. The status is returned as a boolean or string.

* * *

Using Python’s idiomatic style keeps the code concise and readable. The function avoids unnecessary complexity while maintaining clarity. The status is determined efficiently. The function meets the constraints and performs its task well. The status of the input is clearly communicated.
